baseline.notes: Run 50 sporting_ac with field_crop (no upscale, no ball_zoom): F1 0.210, P 0.515, R 0.132, TP 17/129, FP 16. Run 51 (upscale=1440) was a null change (F1 0.221, +0.011). Run 52 adds a second VLM view per moment: the field_crop (wide) + a 640x360 ball-centric zoom when YOLO detects the ball (~35% of frames). VLM sees 5-10 images per window depending on ball detection rate; prompt prefix explains pair structure.

targets.notes: The dual-view is designed to preserve set-piece recall (throw_in/corner/goal_kick, which need field-region context) via the always-emitted wide view, while adding pixel-density gain for dynamic events (shot, save, catch) via the ball zoom. Watch per-type breakdown: throw_in should hold near Run 50 (field view unchanged), dynamic events should improve. Net F1 gain expected: +0.02 to +0.05 if ball_zoom is useful on the ~35% of frames where it fires.

ball_crop_enabled.reason: Run 52 Option A: always emit wide (field_crop) view; additionally emit ball-centric zoom when YOLO detects ball with conf >= 0.15. 5 moments -> 5-10 images per window (variable on ball detection rate). Prompt prefix explains pair structure.

notes: Run #52 (commit 20f862f): ball_crop in dual-view mode. Smoke test confirmed correct interleaving — sporting 3 samples -> 3 wide frames (ball not detected); Rush 4 samples -> 5 frames (1 moment emitted wide+zoom). Ball detection sparsity known: sporting ~35% at conf=0.05, ~25% at conf=0.15. Pre-flight pending.
